mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, breaking the historical unemployment-recession return correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and real new house sales indicate a synchronized collapse in residential demand, which historically precedes broader credit tightening and negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint decline in housing series fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening real economic activity, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative SPX returns in > 55% of observations during easing periods, indicating supply signals are overridden by liquidity shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts implemented while unemployment remains below 5.0% signal proactive monetary easing to prevent a downturn, preserving growth expectations and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rate cuts below 5% unemployment trigger negative returns in > 60% of cases, indicating market interpretation as recessionary rather than preemptive easing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% annualized indicates severe demand contraction, which compresses corporate profit margins and triggers sustained negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0% is followed by positive SPX returns in > 50% of instances, breaking the historical recession-return correlation and invalidating the demand-earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirm a broad-based economic contraction, prompting institutional deleveraging and negative forward equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months AND INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Dual declines in labor and supply series fail to predict negative returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 55% over any 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal accelerating inflation, which raises real discount rates and erodes corporate profit expectations, driving negative short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL is followed by positive SPX returns in > 60% of observations, indicating inflation is being interpreted as demand-driven growth rather than a policy risk.
